name: when-using-flow-nexus-platform-use-flow-nexus-platform
description: Comprehensive Flow Nexus platform management covering authentication, sandboxes, storage, databases, app deployment, payments, and monitoring. This SOP provides end-to-end platform operations.
capabilities: Register and authenticate users with Flow Nexus platform
Create and configure development sandboxes with E2B
Deploy applications from templates or custom code
Monitor system health, logs, and metrics
Configure storage buckets and database schemas
Manage billing, credits, and auto-refill settings
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no code execution capability. All 231 static findings are false positives triggered by markdown documentation containing example code snippets. The skill provides SOP documentation for legitimate Flow Nexus platform operations through official MCP tools.
valueStatement: Flow Nexus provides cloud sandboxes, storage, databases, and deployment services. This skill automates authentication, sandbox creation, application deployment, monitoring, and billing management for the Flow Nexus platform.
limitations: Requires Flow Nexus MCP server installation
Limited to services offered by Flow Nexus platform
Credit balance required for sandbox and deployment usage
Cannot access other cloud platforms directly

useCases.title: Automate cloud infrastructure
useCases.description: Set up development environments, deploy applications, and configure monitoring automatically

useCases.title: Deploy web applications
useCases.description: Deploy Next.js, Node.js, and other templates to cloud sandboxes with one command

useCases.title: Manage platform operations
useCases.description: Monitor system health, track usage, configure alerts, and handle billing centrally

promptTemplates.title: Setup Flow Nexus
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the Flow Nexus platform skill to register a new account and authenticate. Set up the platform configuration and create a development sandbox.

promptTemplates.title: Deploy Application
promptTemplates.prompt: Deploy a Next.js application to Flow Nexus. Use the nextjs-starter template, configure environment variables, and verify the deployment is healthy.

promptTemplates.title: Monitor Resources
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the Flow Nexus system health, review sandbox status and logs, and list any running deployments.

promptTemplates.title: Full Platform Management
promptTemplates.prompt: Run the complete Flow Nexus platform workflow: authenticate, configure services, deploy an application, set up monitoring, and configure billing with auto-refill.

bestPractices: Always verify authentication before performing operations
Store platform configuration in version-controlled files
Use environment variables for sensitive credentials
Configure auto-refill to avoid service interruptions
antiPatterns: Hardcoding credentials in scripts or configuration files
Ignoring monitoring alerts or log warnings

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It requires the Flow Nexus MCP server installed.

faq.question: What are the resource limits?
faq.answer: Default limits include 60 requests per minute, 1000MB storage, and 10 compute hours per user tier.

faq.question: How does MCP integration work?
faq.answer: The skill uses Flow Nexus MCP tools for all platform operations including auth, sandboxes, storage, and billing.

faq.question: Is my data safe with Flow Nexus?
faq.answer: Flow Nexus uses encrypted connections, sandbox isolation, and does not persist credentials beyond the session.

faq.question: Deployment failed, what do I check?
faq.answer: Verify credits balance, check sandbox logs for errors, ensure environment variables are set correctly, and confirm template availability.

faq.question: How is this different from other cloud skills?
faq.answer: Flow Nexus provides integrated sandboxes, storage, databases, and workflows in one platform with unified MCP access.
